source_paper_title: Fast Convex Optimization via Time Scale and Averaging of the Steepest Descent
source_paper_bibtex: @article{Attouch_2025, title={Fast Convex Optimization via Time Scale and Averaging of the Steepest Descent}, volume={50}, ISSN={1526-5471}, url={http://dx.doi.org/10.1287/moor.2023.0186}, DOI={10.1287/moor.2023.0186}, number={4}, journal={Mathematics of Operations Research}, publisher={Institute for Operations Research and the Management Sciences (INFORMS)}, author={Attouch, Hedy and Ioan Boţ, Radu and Nguyen, Dang-Khoa}, year={2025}, month=nov, pages={2633–2665} }
context:
Page 26, Section 5.1 (Remark 8).

It would be interesting to know if we can achieve the same convergence rate using time scaling and perturbation techniques.
title: Achieve faster residual decay for cocoercive-operator Newton-corrected inertial dynamics
background_latex:
Let \(H\) be a real Hilbert space with inner product \(\langle \cdot,\cdot\rangle\) and norm \(\|\cdot\|\). Let \(M:H\to H\) be a single-valued \(\rho\)-cocoercive operator for some \(\rho>0\), meaning
\[
\langle M(x)-M(y),x-y\rangle \ge \rho\,\|M(x)-M(y)\|^2\quad\forall x,y\in H.
\]
Assume the set of zeros \(\mathrm{Zer}
M:=\{x\in H: M(x)=0\}\) is nonempty.

For a trajectory \(y:[s_0,\infty)\to H\) with \(s_0>0\), define the (formal) time derivative of \(M(y(s))\) by the chain rule notation
\[
\frac{d}{ds}(M(y(s)))
\]
(when \(M\) is Lipschitz, this can be understood in the a.e. sense along absolutely continuous \(y\)).

Consider the second-order inertial dynamics with vanishing viscous damping and a Newton-correction term
\[
 y''(s)+\frac{\alpha}{s}y'(s)+c(s)\,\frac{d}{ds}(M(y(s)))+M(y(s))=0,\qquad s\ge s_0,
\]
where \(\alpha>1\) and \(c(s)>0\) is a prescribed scalar coefficient. Two specific choices discussed are
1) \(c(s)=\frac{s}{\alpha+1}\), yielding the system
\[
 y''(s)+\frac{\alpha}{s}y'(s)+\frac{s}{\alpha+1}\,\frac{d}{ds}(M(y(s)))+M(y(s))=0,
\]
for which the paper proves \(\|M(y(s))\|=o(1/s)\).
2) \(c(s)=\frac{2s}{\alpha+1}\), yielding the closely related system
\[
 y''(s)+\frac{\alpha}{s}y'(s)+\frac{2s}{\alpha+1}\,\frac{d}{ds}(M(y(s)))+M(y(s))=0,
\]
which another work reports satisfies the sharper decay \(\|M(y(s))\|=o(1/s^2)\) and \(\int_{s_0}^\infty s^3\|M(y(s))\|^2\,ds<\infty\).
problem_definition_latex:
Determine whether the time-scaling and perturbation approach developed for steepest-descent-type systems can be used to obtain the improved convergence rate
\[
\|M(y(s))\|=o\!\left(\frac{1}{s^2}\right)\quad\text{as }s\to\infty
\]
(and/or the corresponding weighted integrability \(\int_{s_0}^\infty s^3\|M(y(s))\|^2\,ds<\infty\)) for the Newton-corrected cocoercive-operator inertial dynamics
\[
 y''(s)+\frac{\alpha}{s}y'(s)+\frac{s}{\alpha+1}\,\frac{d}{ds}(M(y(s)))+M(y(s))=0,
\]
or for an appropriately modified coefficient \(c(s)\) within the same time-scaling/perturbation framework, under the standing assumptions that \(M\) is cocoercive and \(\mathrm{Zer}\,M\neq\emptyset\).

open_problem_quote: It would be interesting to know if we can achieve the same convergence rate using time scaling and perturbation techniques.
counterexample_suitability_rationale: The question asks for establishing an improved asymptotic rate under broad operator assumptions rather than refuting a concrete conjectured bound. Searching for counterexamples would require exploring infinite-dimensional operator classes and verifying asymptotic rates of ODE solutions, which is not a finite, well-parameterized instance space.
alphaevolve_suitability_rationale: An evolutionary code loop could explore alternative time scalings or coefficient functions \(c(s)\) and test empirically on finite-dimensional examples (e.g., linear cocoercive operators) by numerically integrating the ODE and measuring residual decay. This would not prove the general result but could discover promising scalings/perturbations that exhibit \(1/s^2\) decay and guide analysis.

verification.notice: Generated from the paper text only and not independently verified as still open.

literature_review.synthesis:
In Attouch–Boţ–Nguyen’s time-scaling/averaging program, the cocoercive-operator baseline flow \(z'(t)+M(z(t))=0\) yields only \(\|M(z(t))\|=o(t^{-1/2})\), and after the specific open-loop scaling \(\tau(s)=s^2/(2(\alpha+1))\) one obtains the Newton-corrected inertial dynamics
\[y''(s)+\frac{\alpha}{s}y'(s)+\frac{s}{\alpha+1}\frac{d}{ds}M(y(s))+M(y(s))=0\]
with \(\|M(y(s))\|=o(1/s)\) and \(\int s\|M(y(s))\|^2ds<\infty\) (Theorem 12 of the source paper). The paper explicitly notes (Remark 8) that a closely related modification with coefficient \(\frac{2s}{\alpha+1}\) in front of \(\frac{d}{ds}M(y(s))\) enjoys the sharper \(\|M(y(s))\|=o(1/s^2)\) and \(\int s^3\|M(y(s))\|^2ds<\infty\) in subsequent work, leaving open whether the original time-scaling/perturbation route can recover this sharper decay for the \(\frac{s}{\alpha+1}\)-coefficient model or via a nearby choice of \(c(s)\).

Among forward citations, the strongest evidence that \(o(1/s^2)\) residual decay is achievable within a Newton-corrected operator framework comes from the continuous/discrete Fast OGDA analysis, which studies a more general vanishing-damping Newton-corrected dynamics with a tunable scaling \(\beta(t)\) and an additional \(V\)-term coefficient tied to \(\beta\). It proves \(\|V(z(t))\|=o(1/(t\beta(t)))\) and \(\int t\beta(t)^2\|V(z(t))\|^2dt<\infty\); taking \(\beta(t)\asymp t\) yields exactly \(\|V(z(t))\|=o(1/t^2)\) and \(\int t^3\|V(z(t))\|^2dt<\infty\). This matches the target rate/integrability but for a dynamics that differs from the open-problem equation by the presence of an extra coefficient multiplying \(V(z(t))\), so it does not directly settle the \(c(s)=s/(\alpha+1)\) case.

Other cited works develop complementary time-rescaling equivalences (Heavy Ball \(\leftrightarrow\) Fast OGDA), stochastic time-scaling/averaging transfers, and anchored/Tikhonov regularization equivalences for operator-derivative systems. Collectively they suggest two plausible routes to \(o(1/s^2)\): (i) modify the Newton-correction model so that the coefficient in front of \(M(y(s))\) is coupled to \(c(s)\) (as in Fast OGDA-type templates), enabling Lyapunov cancellations that yield \(o(1/(s c(s)))\); or (ii) embed the dynamics into an equivalent first-order anchored flow (possibly with Tikhonov regularization) where sharper residual decay can be proved and then transferred back by time rescaling. At present, none of the forward-citing papers provides a proof of \(\|M(y(s))\|=o(1/s^2)\) for the exact equation with \(c(s)=s/(\alpha+1)\) under only cocoercivity, so the problem remains open in the stated form.

sources.title: Fast Optimistic Gradient Descent Ascent (OGDA) method in continuous and discrete time
sources.theorem_or_result:
For a monotone continuous operator \(V:H\to H\) with nonempty zero set \(Z\), the paper studies the second-order Newton-corrected vanishing-damping dynamics
\[
\ddot z(t)+\frac{\alpha}{t}\dot z(t)+\beta(t)\frac{d}{dt}V(z(t)) + \frac12\Big(\dot\beta(t)+\frac{\alpha}{t}\beta(t)\Big)V(z(t))=0,\qquad \alpha>2,
\]
with \(\beta\) nondecreasing and \(\sup_{t\ge t_0} \tfrac{t\dot\beta(t)}{\beta(t)}<\alpha-2\). They prove last-iterate rates
- \(\|V(z(t))\|=o\big(\tfrac{1}{t\,\beta(t)}\big)\) and \(\langle z(t)-z^*,V(z(t))\rangle=o\big(\tfrac{1}{t\,\beta(t)}\big)\) for any \(z^*\in Z\),
- weighted integrability \(\int_{t_0}^\infty t\,\beta(t)^2\,\|V(z(t))\|^2\,dt<\infty\),
and weak convergence \(z(t)\rightharpoonup z^*\in Z\). For polynomial \(\beta(t)=\beta_0 t^\rho\) (\(\rho\ge0\)), this yields \(\|V(z(t))\|=o(1/t^{\rho+1})\) and \(\int t^{2\rho+1}\|V(z(t))\|^2<\infty\).
sources.connection:
The open problem asks whether time-scaling/perturbation ideas can upgrade the residual decay for the Newton-corrected inertial dynamics
\(y''+\frac{\alpha}{s}y'+\frac{s}{\alpha+1}\frac{d}{ds}M(y)+M(y)=0\)
from \(\|M(y(s))\|=o(1/s)\) to \(o(1/s^2)\) (and \(\int s^3\|M(y)\|^2<\infty\)) under cocoercivity.

This paper provides a closely related, more general monotone-operator framework with an explicit time-scaling function \(\beta(t)\) and a derivative correction term \(\beta(t)\tfrac{d}{dt}V(z(t))\), proving \(\|V(z(t))\|=o(1/(t\beta(t)))\) and \(\int t\beta(t)^2\|V(z(t))\|^2<\infty\). By choosing \(\beta(t)\asymp t\), one gets \(\|V(z(t))\|=o(1/t^2)\) and \(\int t^3\|V(z(t))\|^2<\infty\), matching the target rate/integrability.

However, the open problem concerns a *specific* dynamics with fixed coefficient \(c(s)=\tfrac{s}{\alpha+1}\) and only cocoercivity (not general monotonicity plus the extra \(\tfrac12(\dot\beta+\tfrac{\alpha}{t}\beta)V\) term). The paper does not show that the original \(c(s)=\tfrac{s}{\alpha+1}\) system can be transformed into their model, nor does it derive \(o(1/s^2)\) for that exact equation. It instead shows that within a modified time-scaling/Newton-correction framework (with an additional \(V\)-term coefficient tied to \(\beta\)), one can indeed achieve \(o(1/t^2)\) by taking \(\beta(t)\sim t\).

sources.title: Recovering Nesterov accelerated dynamics from Heavy Ball dynamics via time rescaling
sources.theorem_or_result:
For a monotone and continuous (in particular, cocoercive) operator \(V:H\to H\), the paper studies the Heavy-Ball-type Newton-corrected dynamics
\[\ddot y(t)+\lambda \dot y(t)+\mu(t)\tfrac{d}{dt}V(y(t))+\gamma(t)V(y(t))=0\]
under the asymptotic ratio condition \(\lim_{t\to\infty}\gamma(t)/\mu(t)=:L\in(0,\infty)\) and growth bounds (Assumption (4.12)). It proves (Theorem 4.3) that any strong global solution satisfies
- \(\|V(y(t))\|=o(1/\mu(t))\), \(\langle y(t)-x^*,V(y(t))\rangle=o(1/\mu(t))\), and \(\|\dot y(t)\|\to 0\),
- \(y(t)\rightharpoonup \bar y\in\mathrm{Zer}\,V\).
Moreover, via time-rescaling equivalence with Fast OGDA (Proposition 5.1), it recovers for the vanishing-damping system
\[\ddot x(s)+\tfrac{\alpha}{s}\dot x(s)+\tfrac{d}{ds}V(x(s))+\tfrac{\alpha}{2s}V(x(s))=0\]
that \(\|V(x(s))\|=o(1/s)\) (Theorem 5.2).
sources.connection:
The open problem asks whether a time-scaling/perturbation framework can upgrade the residual decay for the specific Newton-corrected inertial dynamics
\(y''(s)+\frac{\alpha}{s}y'(s)+\frac{s}{\alpha+1}\frac{d}{ds}M(y(s))+M(y(s))=0\)
(from \(o(1/s)\) to \(o(1/s^2)\)) for a \(\rho\)-cocoercive operator \(M\).

This citing paper develops a closely related time-rescaling program, but for a different family of Newton-corrected operator dynamics: it analyzes a Heavy-Ball-with-constant-friction model with two free scalings \(\mu(t),\gamma(t)\) in front of \(\tfrac{d}{dt}V(y(t))\) and \(V(y(t))\), and then shows it is time-reparametrization equivalent to the Fast OGDA vanishing-damping system with coefficients \(1\) (for \(\tfrac{d}{ds}V\)) and \(\alpha/(2s)\) (for \(V\)). The resulting residual rate is \(\|V(x(s))\|=o(1/s)\), not \(o(1/s^2)\).

Thus it does not solve the stated open problem. However, it is highly relevant because it (i) provides an explicit time-rescaling equivalence for Newton-corrected monotone-operator inertial dynamics, and (ii) gives a general residual estimate \(o(1/\mu(t))\) that suggests how choosing faster-growing \(\mu\) might yield faster residual decay in the constant-friction time variable. What is missing for the open problem is a rescaling/choice of coefficients that maps to the specific \(c(s)=s/(\alpha+1)\) model and yields \(o(1/s^2)\) residual decay in the \(s\)-time variable, as well as the weighted integrability \(\int s^3\|M(y(s))\|^2 ds<\infty\).

sources.title: Stochastic inertial dynamics via time scaling and averaging
sources.theorem_or_result:
The paper develops a time-scaling-and-averaging framework (in a stochastic setting) that transfers convergence properties of first-order stochastic (sub)gradient flows to second-order inertial dynamics with implicit Hessian-driven damping. In particular, for the deterministic/noiseless case and 
- viscous damping \(\gamma(t)=\alpha/t\) with \(\alpha>3\),
- geometric damping \(\beta(t)=t/(\alpha-1)\) (equivalently \(\beta\equiv \Gamma\) for the associated \(\Gamma\)),
under suitable integrability of the diffusion term (or \(\sigma\equiv 0\)), they obtain fast rates for the inertial system
\[\ddot X(t)+\tfrac{\alpha}{t}\dot X(t)+\nabla f\bigl(X(t)+\beta(t)\dot X(t)\bigr)=\text{noise},\]
including \(f(X(t)) - \min f = o(1/t^2)\) a.s. and \(\int_{t_0}^\infty t^3\|\nabla f(X(t)+\beta(t)\dot X(t))\|^2\,dt<\infty\) a.s. (Corollary 3.5).
sources.connection: The open problem asks whether the time-scaling/perturbation approach from Attouch–Bot–Nguyen can yield the sharper residual decay \(\|M(y(s))\|=o(1/s^2)\) (and weighted integrability) for the Newton-corrected inertial dynamics with a cocoercive operator term \(c(s)\tfrac{d}{ds}M(y(s))\). This citing paper does not analyze that Newton-corrected cocoercive-operator ODE. Instead it treats (stochastic) inertial dynamics of implicit Hessian-driven type, where the ‘Newton correction’ is implicit: \(\nabla f(x+\beta\dot x)\), not an explicit \(\tfrac{d}{ds}M(y(s))\) term. Nevertheless, it provides strong evidence that time scaling + averaging can upgrade rates from \(o(1/s)\) to \(o(1/s^2)\) for residual-like quantities in related second-order systems, and it explicitly mentions as a future direction extension to non-potential cocoercive operators. Thus it is partial progress/related methodology rather than a solution: it supplies a transferable technique and achieves the desired \(o(1/t^2)\)-type rate for gradients in the gradient/subgradient setting, but does not establish \(o(1/s^2)\) for \(\|M(y(s))\|\) in the specific Newton-corrected cocoercive-operator dynamics with \(c(s)=s/(\alpha+1)\).

sources.title: Tikhonov regularization of monotone operator flows not only ensures strong convergence of the trajectories but also speeds up the vanishing of the residuals
sources.theorem_or_result:
The paper proves that adding Tikhonov regularization (and, via time-rescaling/anchoring equivalences, certain first-order flows) yields strong convergence and improved residual/velocity decay. In particular, for the first-order regularized flow \(x'(t)+M(x(t)) + \tfrac{1}{t}x(t)=0\) with monotone continuous \(M\) and \(\mathrm{Zer}
M\neq\emptyset\), it establishes \(\|x'(t)\|=O(1/t)\) and \(\|M(x(t))\|=O(1/t)\) and strong convergence \(x(t)\to P_{\mathrm{Zer}M}(0)\). It also shows an equivalence between a second-order vanishing-damping/derivative-of-operator system \(z''(s)+\tfrac{\alpha}{s}z'(s)+\beta(s)\tfrac{d}{ds}M(z(s))+(\beta'(s)+\tfrac{\beta(s)}{s})M(z(s))=0\) and a first-order anchored system \(z'(s)+\beta(s)M(z(s)) + \tfrac{\alpha-1}{s}(z(s)-v)=0\), yielding residual rates such as \(\|M(z(s))\|=O(1/s^{\alpha})\) when \(\inf_{s\ge s_0} s\beta'(s)/\beta(s) > \alpha-2\).
sources.connection: The open problem asks whether time-scaling/perturbation methods can upgrade the residual decay for the Newton-corrected inertial dynamics \(y''+\tfrac{\alpha}{s}y' + \tfrac{s}{\alpha+1}\tfrac{d}{ds}M(y)+M(y)=0\) from \(\|M(y(s))\|=o(1/s)\) to \(o(1/s^2)\) (or weighted integrability). This citing paper does not analyze that specific coefficient choice \(c(s)=\tfrac{s}{\alpha+1}\) nor prove \(o(1/s^2)\) for it. However, it develops a time-rescaling framework and, crucially, shows that a related class of second-order dynamics with a \(\tfrac{d}{ds}M\) term (equation (3.1)) is equivalent to a first-order anchored/Tikhonov-regularized flow, from which strong convergence and explicit residual rates follow. These techniques are conceptually close to the open problem’s requested “time-scaling/perturbation framework,” and they provide fast residual decay (e.g., \(O(1/s^{\alpha})\) for suitable \(\beta\)), but they do not directly yield the desired \(o(1/s^2)\) for the particular Newton-corrected system in the problem statement.

sources.title: Long-Time Analysis of Stochastic Heavy Ball Dynamics for Convex Optimization and Monotone Equations
sources.theorem_or_result:
For a monotone and Lipschitz operator \(V:H\to H\) with \(\mathrm{zer}
V\neq\emptyset\), the paper analyzes stochastic Heavy Ball systems with a correction term involving the time derivative of \(V\). Via time-rescaling, it derives fast rates for the stochastic Fast OGDA dynamics
\[z''(s)+\frac{\alpha}{s}z'(s)+\beta\frac{d}{ds}V(z(s))+\frac{\alpha\beta}{2s}V(z(s))=0\]
(and its SDE version), proving in particular (a.s.) \(\|V(Z(s))\|=o(1/s)\) and \(\langle Z(s)-z^*,V(Z(s))\rangle=o(1/s)\) for \(\alpha>2\), plus corresponding \(O(1/s)\) or \(O(1/s^2)\) bounds in expectation for squared residuals/velocities.
sources.connection:
The open problem asks whether the time-scaling/perturbation approach can upgrade the residual decay for the Newton-corrected inertial dynamics
\[y''(s)+\frac{\alpha}{s}y'(s)+\frac{s}{\alpha+1}\,\frac{d}{ds}M(y(s))+M(y(s))=0\]
from \(\|M(y(s))\|=o(1/s)\) to \(o(1/s^2)\) (or weighted integrability), under cocoercivity.

This citing paper is highly thematically aligned: it explicitly uses time-rescaling to transfer convergence-rate results between heavy-ball-with-friction systems and vanishing-damping systems, and it treats monotone equations with a correction term involving \(\frac{d}{dt}V(y(t))\). However, the specific fast-rate result it transfers for monotone equations is for the Fast OGDA-type dynamics
\[z''(s)+\frac{\alpha}{s}z'(s)+\beta\frac{d}{ds}V(z(s))+\frac{\alpha\beta}{2s}V(z(s))=0,\]
which yields residual \(o(1/s)\), not \(o(1/s^2)\). It does not analyze the Newton-corrected coefficient choice \(c(s)=\frac{s}{\alpha+1}\) (or \(\frac{2s}{\alpha+1}\)) nor does it prove \(o(1/s^2)\) residual decay or \(\int s^3\|M(y(s))\|^2<\infty\) for that system.

So it does not solve the open problem, but it provides partial progress/nearby results and a concrete time-scaling framework in the operator (non-potential) setting that could be adapted when trying to tune coefficients to improve residual decay.

sources.title: Fast Krasnoselskii-Mann Method with Overrelaxation and Preconditioners
sources.theorem_or_result:
For the continuous-time second-order system
\[\ddot x(t)+\frac{\alpha}{t}\dot x(t)+\beta(t)\frac{d}{dt}Q(x(t))+b(t)Q(x(t))=0\]
with monotone Lipschitz operator \(Q\) and \(b(t)=(1-\eta)\dot\beta(t)+\theta\beta(t)/t\) where \(\eta\in(0,1)\) and \(\theta=(1-\eta)+\eta(\alpha-1)\), Theorem 2.2 proves (under growth condition \(0\le \dot\beta(t)t/\beta(t)\le \alpha-2-\varepsilon\)) that
- \(\|Q(x(t))\|=O(1/(t\beta(t)))\), and if \(\alpha>2\) and \(\varepsilon>0\) then \(\|Q(x(t))\|=o(1/(t\beta(t)))\) and \(\int_{t_0}^\infty t\beta(t)^2\|Q(x(t))\|^2dt<\infty\).
In discrete time, Algorithm 1 (generalized fast Krasnosel’skii–Mann) yields for nonexpansive \(T\): \(\|x_k-T(x_k)\|=o(1/k)\) and \(\sum_k k\|x_k-T(x_k)\|^2<\infty\) (Theorem 3.3).
sources.connection:
The open problem asks whether a time-scaling/perturbation framework can upgrade the residual decay for the Newton-corrected inertial dynamics
\(y''+\frac{\alpha}{s}y'+\frac{s}{\alpha+1}\frac{d}{ds}M(y)+M(y)=0\)
with \(M\) cocoercive from \(\|M(y(s))\|=o(1/s)\) to \(o(1/s^2)\) (and \(\int s^3\|M(y)\|^2<\infty\)).
This citing paper develops Lyapunov/time-scaling analysis for a closely related class
\(\ddot x+\frac{\alpha}{t}\dot x+\beta(t)\frac{d}{dt}Q(x)+b(t)Q(x)=0\)
with monotone Lipschitz \(Q\), proving \(\|Q(x(t))\|=o(1/(t\beta(t)))\) and weighted integrability \(\int t\beta(t)^2\|Q\|^2<\infty\). However, it does not treat the specific coefficient choice \(\beta(t)=t/(\alpha+1)\) (which would violate their growth condition unless \(\alpha\ge 3\) and still gives only \(o(1/t^2)\) at best), nor does it analyze the exact Newton-corrected cocoercive-operator dynamics in the problem statement. It therefore does not resolve the open problem, but provides a relevant Lyapunov/time-scaling template and shows how choosing faster-growing \(\beta\) can yield faster residual decay in related systems.
